preview_text:
The model favours Uzbekistan at 46%, with DR Congo at 24% and draws at 30%. Uzbekistan's forecast edge is substantial but not commanding; the model assigns nearly one-in-four odds to a DR Congo victory and rates a stalemate as the single most likely outcome class.

The 30-point spread between Uzbekistan's win probability and a draw suggests the match sits in a moderately competitive band. The model implies this fixture carries meaningful upset potential—a DR Congo win would rank as a clear upset, yet the 24% probability indicates it remains plausible rather than remote. A draw appears likelier than either team's outright victory, pointing toward a tightly matched contest where neither side is heavily favoured to break the deadlock.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices align closely with each other but diverge sharply from the model. Books and traders assign DR Congo a 55–56% win probability versus the model's 24%, a gap of roughly 31 percentage points. Conversely, both market sources place Uzbekistan at around 21%, well below the model's 46%. This dashboard presents both forecasts without claiming either is correct; the large divergence suggests material disagreement about match dynamics.
